Title: On the Role of Hydrogen Migrations in the Taxadiene System

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class I diterpene synthase; terpene cyclase
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: taxa-4,11-diene; taxa-4(20),11-diene; taxaxenene; cyclophomactene; isocembrene A2; isocembrene C; verticilla-3,8(19),12(18)-triene; taxasimplene
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Taxa-4,11-diene is the committed hydrocarbon precursor of paclitaxel (taxol) biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: taxa-4,11-diene (major); taxa-4(20),11-diene (minor) from GGPP; taxaxenene from iso-GGPP I
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GGPP into taxadiene skeletons via carbocation cascade with long-range proton migrations; also converts the substrate analogue iso-GGPP I into taxaxenene and other novel diterpene hydrocarbons
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Committed-step enzyme for entry into the taxane skeleton; rate-limiting not established in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Determines formation of taxadiene-system diterpene products and reveals that long-range proton migrations govern product outcome
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme assays with purified truncated His-tagged enzyme; site-directed mutagenesis; isotopic labeling; GC/MS; NMR; DFT calculations
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed as soluble protein in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assays with GGPP or iso-GGPP I; isotopically labeled substrates; D2O buffer for selected assays
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Streptomyces cyaneofuscatus GGPPS; Escherichia coli IDI
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Active-site substitutions often caused inactivity or strongly reduced production; product profile readily shifts to off-pathway diterpenes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Study used a truncated enzyme lacking 78 N-terminal amino acids and carrying a His tag, referred to as TxS; X-ray structure PDB 3P5R used for mutagenesis design
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Truncated TxS converted GGPP mainly to taxa-4,11-diene (87%) and taxa-4(20),11-diene (13%). 2. Isotopic labeling and DFT supported long-range proton migrations during TxS-catalyzed cyclization in the taxadiene system. 3. TxS also converted iso-GGPP I to the novel diterpene taxaxenene, providing experimental support for proton migration from C10 to C2 in a taxadiene-related system.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Mutant class I diterpene synthase; terpene cyclase
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Alternative cyclization branch from carbocation intermediate C
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Diverts flux toward alternative diterpene products
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Mutant TxS redirects the carbocation cascade from GGPP to form cyclophomactene through a pathway involving 1,2-hydride shift, 1,2-methyl migration, long-range proton transfer, and ring closures
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Branch-switch enzyme variant; highest computed barrier toward cyclophomactene was the H-to-J transformation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables production of the new diterpene cyclophomactene, revealing a concerted long-range proton shift/ring-closure mechanism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Site-directed mutagenesis; in vitro enzyme assay; product isolation; NMR structure elucidation; GC/MS; isotopic labeling with deuterated DMAPP-derived GGPP; DFT calculations
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in E. coli for in vitro assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Assays with labeled and unlabeled GGPP substrates
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced overall productivity compared with wild type; major flux redirected away from taxadiene
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also showed good selectivity for verticilla-3,7,12-triene (4) and formed cyclophomactene as a side product that was isolated and characterized
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. G606A altered the TxS product spectrum and enabled isolation of the new diterpene cyclophomactene. 2. Labeling and DFT supported a C17-to-C6 long-range proton transfer and unusual concerted cyclization in cyclophomactene formation. 3. This variant demonstrates that minor active-site remodeling can uncover hidden taxane-related reactivity.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Mutant class I diterpene synthase; terpene cyclase
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Diverts flux away from taxadiene branch
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: cembrene D; verticilla-3,7,12-triene; cembrene A; isocembrene A2; isocembrene C; verticilla-3,8(19),12(18)-triene; taxasimplene
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: With GGPP: verticilla-3,7,12-triene, cembrene A, and cembrene D; with iso-GGPP I: isocembrene A2, isocembrene C, verticilla-3,8(19),12(18)-triene, and taxasimplene
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Mutation abolishes normal taxadiene formation and truncates or reroutes the carbocation cascade to cembrane-, verticillane-, and novel iso-GGPP-derived skeletons
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-switching mutant affecting long-range proton migration; not established as natural rate-limiting step
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Completely loses taxa-4,11-diene production and strongly interferes with long-distance proton migrations, yielding alternative diterpenes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Site-directed mutagenesis; in vitro enzyme assays; GC/MS; product isolation; NMR; stereochemical analysis using deuterated iso-GGPP substrates
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in E. coli for in vitro assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Assays with GGPP and iso-GGPP I substrates
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Eliminates desired taxadiene production and favors off-pathway hydrocarbons
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Selected for further iso-GGPP I studies because it showed the best diterpene production among new variants despite altered specificity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. V610T completely abolished production of taxa-4,11-diene from GGPP and instead produced alternative diterpenes including cembrene D. 2. With iso-GGPP I, V610T generated four major new diterpenes: isocembrene A2, isocembrene C, verticilla-3,8(19),12(18)-triene, and taxasimplene. 3. The mutation strongly perturbs long-range proton migration steps and interrupts the cascade before normal taxane formation.
